text.title: graph-rag-retrieval Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure: classify → route → expand (if hybrid) → assemble → return. Each step names input, action, output, and the decision-gate that pushes the request to the fallback.

step.name: classify-query
step.action: One LLM call with the classifier prompt (templates/classify-query-prompt.txt); temperature=0; return one of GLOBAL/ENTITY/RELATIONSHIP/LOCAL.
step.decision-gate: If parsing fails, set query_type=LOCAL and record fallback_used=true.

step.name: route-by-type
step.action: Dispatch to retrieval path: GLOBAL → read summaries[global]; ENTITY → extract entity, lookup graph neighbors; RELATIONSHIP → extract two entities, shortest_path with try/except; LOCAL → vector_store.search.
step.decision-gate: If ENTITY entity not in graph OR RELATIONSHIP path-not-found → step 5 fallback.

step.name: hybrid-expand
step.input: raw candidates (for ENTITY or LOCAL routes only)
step.action: Extract entities from candidate chunks, expand by graph neighbors (cap 3 per entity), retrieve chunks mentioning expanded entity set with vector_store.search(entity, k=2).
step.decision-gate: If expansion adds 0 new chunks, skip and proceed with raw candidates.

step.name: assemble-context
step.action: Deduplicate by chunk_id, sort by score, truncate to top-k (default k=10), format with source markers.

step.name: fallback-and-return
step.action: If primary path returned 0 candidates, run vector_store.search(query, k=5), mark fallback_used=true.
